title: ELK Stack Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Centralized Logging, Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Filebeat.

questions.question: What is the primary problem that the ELK Stack is designed to solve in a cloud architecture?

options.text: It prevents Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks.
It acts as a primary relational database for processing financial transactions.
It provides a mechanism to deploy code across multiple servers simultaneously.
It centralizes logs from dozens of distributed servers into a single searchable repository.

questions.question: Which component of the ELK Stack acts as a highly scalable search engine capable of performing full-text searches across terabytes of log data?

options.text: Cassandra
Kibana
Elasticsearch
Logstash

questions.question: What does a lightweight agent like Filebeat do when deployed on a web server?

options.text: It compiles the source code locally.
It intercepts network traffic to block unauthorized IPs.
It scans the logs for credit card numbers and encrypts them.
It simply tails local log files on the disk and forwards the data quickly to a central logging pipeline.

questions.question: Which ELK component provides the web-based graphical user interface for engineers to type search queries and view dashboards?

options.text: Elasticsearch
Grafana
Logstash
Kibana

questions.question: What is 'Structured Logging', a modern best practice?

options.text: Logging only when an Error occurs, omitting Info and Debug.
Encrypting the log files so they cannot be read with traditional tools.
Configuring your application to output logs explicitly formatted as JSON rather than raw text strings, enabling faster ingestion.
Sending logs via email strictly to administrators.
